title: Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron sued for alleged RAM price manipulation
summary: Chip manufacturers face a class action lawsuit in the U.S. for restricting the supply of DDR3 and DDR4 memories to inflate prices in a coordinated manner.

title: First gaming PCs with SteamOS preinstalled hit the market
summary: Valve's gaming operating system makes the leap to desktop computers with the help of several hardware manufacturers.

title: CachyOS releases new versions with significant performance and support improvements
summary: The Linux distribution optimized for gaming includes new features like Hyprland Noctalia and extended PGO builds.

title: Ford rehires more than 300 veteran engineers after proving AI couldn't match their expertise
summary: The automaker replaced AI systems with human engineers after seeing they couldn't achieve the same performance.

title: Rocket Lab acquires satellite company Iridium to expand its space business
summary: The company continues its growth strategy through acquisitions in the satellite communications sector.

title: Samsung and SK Hynix to invest more than 500 billion dollars in new chip factories
summary: The plan includes four new plants and HBM memory production centers with the support of the South Korean government.

title: Central bankers warn that the boom in AI investment could trigger a financial crisis
summary: The Bank for International Settlements warns of the risk of a bubble due to massive spending on artificial intelligence without clear returns.

title: Australia raises fines to 99 million dollars for allowing minors to use social media
summary: The Australian government tightens sanctions in light of evidence that children continue to access platforms forbidden by law.

title: Netflix will require a separate email address for each user profile
summary: The platform strengthens its fight against shared accounts by making each profile's identification independent from the account holder's email.

title: Microsoft launches Azure Linux 4.0, its free distribution of the operating system
summary: The company releases a new version of its own Linux, marking a shift from its former stance against open-source software.

title: Chinese Steam Machine clone offers impossible specs at a suspiciously low price
summary: An imitation of Valve's console appears on the market with technical features that seem too good to be true.

title: Digi confirms its IPO to finance fiber and mobile deployment
summary: The Spanish operator seeks to raise funds on the stock market to continue expanding its telecommunications network.

title: U.S. Supreme Court allows firing of FTC commissioners without cause
summary: The court removes the independence of the regulatory agency by allowing the president to dismiss its members, affecting consumer protection and competition.

title: U.S. military used AI to select targets without detecting that one was a school
summary: An AI system overlooked a note warning that one of the selected targets was an educational center.

title: Bernie Sanders proposes a $7 trillion plan for the government to control artificial intelligence
summary: The U.S. senator presents a proposal for AI to be managed by the state for the benefit of citizens.

title: China achieves record hollow-core fiber optic data transmission without signal regeneration
summary: The experiment reached 51.3 Tb/s over 128 miles, a milestone for telecommunications networks in the era of artificial intelligence.

title: SpaceX enters millions of retirement funds after changes in key stock indices
summary: Elon Musk's space company joins indices that open the door to massive investments in its capital from pension funds.

title: PlayStation records its worst May in 25 years and Xbox its worst historical month in sales
summary: Console sales suffer an unprecedented drop during the fifth month of the year according to data gathered from the industry.

title: Nintendo Switch 2 price rises 17% in South Korea starting in September
summary: Nintendo's console experiences a significant increase in the South Korean market due to adjustments in its sales price.

title: Microsoft removes 119 Edge extensions that hid malware in images and fonts
summary: The malicious extensions used graphic files and typographies to hide harmful code without being detected by users.
